name: Altruist
ai_strategy_summary: Altruist's launch of Hazel — the first AI agent dedicated to tax planning for advisors — triggered a $150 billion wealth management stock sell-off in February 2026. The market's reaction signals investor belief that AI will structurally commoditize high-fee advisory services.
headline_metric: $150B market sell-off on Hazel AI launch
headline_initiative: Hazel AI — first dedicated AI tax planning agent for advisors

advisor_productivity.headline: Hazel: AI platform for wealth professionals — meeting intelligence, live custodial data, and tax planning in one environment
advisor_productivity.detail: Altruist launched Hazel in September 2025 (platform) and February 2026 (AI tax planning). Hazel unifies real-time custodial data with CRM, emails, and meeting notes — the first AI platform to combine a major custodian integration with advisor workflow. Tax planning features analyse tax returns and generate personalised strategies in minutes. Hazel is live and in production for RIA advisors on the Altruist platform.
evidence: Hazel platform launched September 2025 — first AI platform to unify custodial data with advisor workflow (Altruist)
AI tax planning launched February 2026 — analyses 1040s, paystubs, account statements to generate personalised tax plans in minutes (Altruist)
Live for RIA advisors on Altruist custodial platform
$152M Series F (GIC, April 2025) — explicitly to fund AI platform expansion
advisor_productivity.jpm_implication: If tax planning is commoditized by AI, the value proposition of human financial advisors narrows further. JPM advisor value articulation must evolve.
